steps.content: If statements allow your program to make decisions. They check if a condition is true and execute code accordingly.

steps.instruction: Create an if statement that checks if a number is greater than 10:
steps.starterCode:
-- Create a number variable
-- Use if statement to check if it's greater than 10
-- Print a message
steps.solution:
local number = 15
if number > 10 then
  print("The number is greater than 10!")
end
steps.hint: Use 'if condition then' to start an if statement

steps.instruction: Add an else clause to handle when the condition is false:
steps.starterCode:
-- Modify your if statement
-- Add an else clause
-- Print different messages
steps.solution:
local number = 5
if number > 10 then
  print("The number is greater than 10!")
else
  print("The number is 10 or less!")
end
steps.hint: Add 'else' after the if block to handle the false case

successMessage: 🎯 Excellent! You can now make your programs think!
